_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 7-8(S) Double Sequential Defibrillation (Revised 3/17/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2025). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
condition: Double Sequential Defibrillation (DSD) — Refractory Ventricular Fibrillation
protocol_reference: Michigan Procedures Protocol Section 7-8(S): Double Sequential Defibrillation — MDHHS Approved 3/17/2025
purpose: PARAMEDIC USE ONLY. MCA OPTIONAL — only available if MCA has authorized in Electrical Therapy protocol. Double sequential defibrillation (DSD) uses two defibrillators fired in rapid sequence for refractory ventricular fibrillation after standard defibrillation has failed at least 3 times. Requires 100% QA/QI review.

sections.title: Eligibility Criteria
points: MCA must have authorized Double Sequential Defibrillation in MCA Electrical Therapy Protocol selection
At least 3 prior defibrillation attempts have been delivered without successful termination of V-fib
A second defibrillator must be available on scene
Standard cardiac arrest management must continue throughout (HQCPR, medications per Cardiac Arrest Protocol)

sections.title: Device Setup and Pad Placement
points: Defibrillator 1 (Defib 1): anterior/posterior (A/P) position — anterior pad placed to the LEFT of the sternum; posterior pad below left scapula
Defibrillator 2 (Defib 2): anterior/lateral (A/L) position — anterior pad placed to the RIGHT of the sternum; lateral pad at left anterior axillary line at the level of the cardiac apex
Ensure pads from Defib 1 and Defib 2 do not overlap or touch
If an AED is being used as one of the two defibrillators: the AED fires FIRST in the sequence

sections.title: Firing Sequence
points: A SINGLE PARAMEDIC fires both defibrillators in rapid sequence — the interval between the two shocks should be less than 1 second
The paramedic must confirm ALL personnel are clear before firing either device
If one device is an AED: AED fires first; manual defibrillator fires immediately after
Resume HQCPR immediately after both shocks are delivered
Repeat DSD at 2-minute intervals if V-fib persists and patient remains eligible
Continue standard cardiac arrest protocol medications and interventions between DSD cycles

sections.title: Quality Assurance and Documentation
points: 100% Quality Assurance/Quality Improvement (QA/QI) review is required for every DSD event
Document each DSD event as TWO separate defibrillations (same time) in the PCR
Narrative MUST include the phrase 'double sequential' or 'dual sequential' defibrillation
Document: number of prior standard defibrillations, reason DSD was initiated, device positions used, times of DSD delivery, patient response

deterioration_flags: V-fib persists after DSD — repeat DSD at 2-minute intervals; continue HQCPR and antidysrhythmics per Cardiac Arrest Protocol
Only one defibrillator available — no DSD; vector change (A/P to A/L or vice versa) for refractory V-fib; contact Medical Control
scope_notes: MCA OPTIONAL — only authorized if MCA has selected DSD in Electrical Therapy Protocol
Requires ≥ 3 prior shocks AND second defibrillator on scene
Defib 1: A/P (anterior left of sternum); Defib 2: A/L (anterior right of sternum + lateral left axillary)
AED fires first if used as one of the two defibrillators
Both devices fired in rapid sequence (< 1 second) by a single paramedic
100% QA/QI review required for every DSD event
Document as 2 defibrillations same time; narrative must include 'double sequential' or 'dual sequential'
